text.title: Laravel Framework Fundamentals Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Laravel codebases.

antipattern.symptom: Local works, production crashes with 'env null' after `php artisan config:cache`.
antipattern.root-cause: `env('STRIPE_KEY')` called inside a service class (not in `config/*.php`).
antipattern.fix: Move env() calls to `config/stripe.php` (`return ['key' => env('STRIPE_KEY')];`); reference via `config('stripe.key')` in services.

antipattern.symptom: Tests need `Mail::fake()`, `Queue::fake()`, `Storage::fake()` everywhere; setUp() is 30 lines of fake calls.
antipattern.root-cause: Services use facades directly; mocking requires Laravel's fake registry.
antipattern.fix: Refactor services to constructor-inject contracts (MailService, QueueService); unit tests use plain PHPUnit mocks.

antipattern.symptom: `php artisan route:cache` fails with closure-not-serializable error.
antipattern.root-cause: Route uses inline closure with `use ($variable)`; closures can't be cached.
antipattern.fix: Convert inline closures to controller method references: `Route::get('/', [HomeController::class, 'index'])`.

antipattern.symptom: Service binding only works after a specific endpoint is hit; first request after deploy fails.
antipattern.root-cause: `app()->bind(MailService::class, ...)` placed inside a controller; not loaded at boot.
antipattern.fix: Move binding to `register()` in a ServiceProvider; register provider in `config/app.php`.
